title: Scaling Laws Under SuperBEST v5 (add = 2n)
breakthrough: add(x,y) = 2n for ALL real x,y via LEdiv(x, DEML(y,1))

scaling_law_updates.name: N-term positive sum
scaling_law_updates.note: add_pos 3n → 2n; N-1 additions now cost 2n each

scaling_law_updates.name: N-term general signed sum
scaling_law_updates.note: Massive improvement: signed sums are now as cheap as positive sums

structural_insight: The signed/unsigned split that defined two classes of equations is eliminated. The 8n gap (add_gen=11n vs add_pos=3n) was the primary cost driver for Class D (mixed-sign) equations. With add=2n for all reals, Class D equations drop by up to 9n per addition, completely closing the gap with Class A/B/C equations in terms of per-addition cost.

class_averages_estimate.note: Estimated change; exact numbers require full catalog rescan
class_averages_estimate.class_D_change: Most affected — each add_gen=11n becomes 2n, saving 9n per term

predict_cost_impact.all_predictions_using_add_gen: Need rescan — equations using add_gen get cheaper predicted cost
predict_cost_impact.validation_status: Requires re-run of predict_cost on full 315-equation catalog

completeness_statement: SuperBEST v5 is the complete table. All 10 operations cost <= 3n. No outliers. The positive-domain/general-domain split is eliminated. The table cannot be improved without changing the operator family F16.
